mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following a >0.5pp unemployment spike, invalidating the risk-off transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle expansions, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases show no negative predictive power for 5-day forward returns over a 24-month rolling window, with directional accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls crossing above 130 million confirms sustained labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive when PAYEMS exceeds 130 million, with a hit rate below 50% across all late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth outpacing federal funds rate increases signals an accommodative financial environment relative to economic expansion, supporting equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 growth and FEDFUNDS changes yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day returns during easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment indicates a supply-side contraction, forcing immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a directional accuracy above 55% during recessionary onsets.
